title: The Digital Choices Shaping our Children’s Health
content: Around the world, childhood is being reprogrammed by digital technologies that shape how young people learn, play, and connect. But children and young people are not experimental subjects, a captive market, or a commodity, which is why we must shape digital environments that protect and support their healthy development.

creator: Emmanuel Macron
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Oligarchy’s Ancient Origins
content: The struggle between democracy and oligarchy in classical Greece was bitter, with oligarchies relying on skillfully designed political institutions to shore up unpopular regimes. As concerns grow over the “rule of the few” in the United States, Americans should consider how their own institutions are doing oligarchs’ work for them.
snippet: The struggle between democracy and oligarchy in classical Greece was bitter, with oligarchies relying on skillfully designed political institutions to shore up unpopular regimes. As concerns grow over
creator: Matt Simonton
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Modi Has Remade India
content: Over the last 12 years, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has overseen dizzying progress in economic modernization and state capacity-building, as well as dismaying setbacks on institutional independence and minority inclusion. This record offers important insights into how India will navigate the 21st century.
snippet: Over the last 12 years, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has overseen dizzying progress in economic modernization and state capacity-building, as well as dismaying setbacks on institutional
creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Water Makes the World Go Round
content: Despite being vital to health, security, and economic prosperity, water is often taken for granted. As supplies come under growing strain, water-related considerations will shape economic policy and political decision-making, as well as firms’ investment decisions, risk assessments, and corporate strategies.
snippet: Despite being vital to health, security, and economic prosperity, water is often taken for granted. As supplies come under growing strain, water-related considerations will shape economic policy and
creator: Esther Crauser-Delbourg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real Competitiveness Trap
content: Policymakers obsessed with competitiveness both in Europe and beyond need to abandon old orthodoxies. New research finds that for two decades, capital in Europe has been abundant and cheap, and corporate profits strong, yet investment, productivity, and wages have stalled, and labor's share of total income has shrunk.
snippet: Policymakers obsessed with competitiveness both in Europe and beyond need to abandon old orthodoxies. New research finds that for two decades, capital in Europe has been abundant and cheap, and
creator: Mariana Mazzucato
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How to Empower Africa’s Multilateral Development Banks
content: Capitalizing multilateral development banks in Africa is a strategic investment in the continent’s economic transformation. To mobilize adequate funding will require leveraging domestic savings, pursuing monetary integration, using innovative financing mechanisms, and strengthening regional capital markets.
snippet: Capitalizing multilateral development banks in Africa is a strategic investment in the continent’s economic transformation. To mobilize adequate funding will require leveraging domestic savings,
creator: Hippolyte Fofack
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Americans Are Wary of AI
content: The tech industry is asking Americans to embrace AI at a moment when trust in government is declining. While these may seem like separate issues, persuading people to trust AI will be far more difficult if they do not trust the institutions responsible for regulating it.
snippet: The tech industry is asking Americans to embrace AI at a moment when trust in government is declining. While these may seem like separate issues, persuading people to trust AI will be far more
creator: Ngaire Woods
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why the Gulf Needs Economic Ties with Iran
content: Most Gulf observers agree that the old security order is dying. But none of the most commonly proposed options—buying more advanced weapons, seeking protection from new partners in the Global South, or building stronger national militaries—is sufficient on its own.
snippet: Most Gulf observers agree that the old security order is dying. But none of the most commonly proposed options—buying more advanced weapons, seeking protection from new partners in the Global South,
creator: Adeel Malik
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Right Way to Tackle Developing Countries’ Cancer Crisis
content: Some health advocates believe that weakening intellectual-property protections and capping prices for the most advanced cancer treatments would expand access in developing countries. But this well-intentioned argument overlooks an inescapable reality: without strong incentives for innovation, such treatments would not exist.
snippet: Some health advocates believe that weakening intellectual-property protections and capping prices for the most advanced cancer treatments would expand access in developing countries. But this
creator: Thompson Ayodele
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Enforce AI Guardrails Before It’s Too Late
content: The Trump administration’s decision to impose export controls on Anthropic’s latest models is emblematic of the incoherent and shifting response from policymakers worldwide to the rapidly advancing AI sector. To ensure that these tools do not court disaster, governments must establish meaningful guardrails.
snippet: The Trump administration’s decision to impose export controls on Anthropic’s latest models is emblematic of the incoherent and shifting response from policymakers worldwide to the rapidly advancing AI
creator: Gabriela Ramos
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is a European Single Market for Energy Such a Good Idea?
content: Although textbook microeconomics shows why less energy-market fragmentation would be better for Europe as a whole, that does not mean it would be a good deal for countries producing low-carbon electricity at minimal marginal cost. In fact, if more integration creates free riders, the political backlash could be profound.
snippet: Although textbook microeconomics shows why less energy-market fragmentation would be better for Europe as a whole, that does not mean it would be a good deal for countries producing low-carbon
creator: Brigitte Granville
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China’s Failed Rebalancing
content: When China’s leaders first acknowledged the need to rebalance the economy nearly two decades ago, it seemed like a matter of when, not if. But with the household consumption share of Chinese GDP remaining stubbornly low, officials’ promises to boost domestic demand have lost all credibility.
snippet: When China’s leaders first acknowledged the need to rebalance the economy nearly two decades ago, it seemed like a matter of when, not if. But with the household consumption share of Chinese GDP
creator: Stephen S. Roach
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Crowning a Healthy Populism
content: Although recent political mobilizations in Hungary, Albania, and other parts of Central and Eastern Europe could be described as populist and nationalist, they have little in common with the aggressive ethnonationalism that MAGA professes. Could they be a model for a new form of politics?
snippet: Although recent political mobilizations in Hungary, Albania, and other parts of Central and Eastern Europe could be described as populist and nationalist, they have little in common with the
creator: Harold James
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Rules-Based Orders Fail
content: For a brief period after the Cold War, Americans persuaded themselves that the liberal order had become self-sustaining. And yet, any rules-based system elaborate enough to govern and interpret its own operations will eventually confront questions that its rules cannot answer.
snippet: For a brief period after the Cold War, Americans persuaded themselves that the liberal order had become self-sustaining. And yet, any rules-based system elaborate enough to govern and interpret its
creator: Benn Steil
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Tide Is Turning Against Russia
content: With Ukraine reclaiming territory, mounting drone strikes deep in Russian territory, and draining Russia’s coffers, Vladimir Putin would do well to seek a negotiated freeze to the conflict. But there is no guarantee that he will, not least because he might not grasp the situation Russia is in.
snippet: With Ukraine reclaiming territory, mounting drone strikes deep in Russian territory, and draining Russia’s coffers, Vladimir Putin would do well to seek a negotiated freeze to the conflict. But there
creator: Sergei Guriev
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Managing Sino-American Interdependence
content: At their summit last month, US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping seemed to recognize that they are in the same boat. If either the United States or China tries to cripple or decouple from the other, it will harm its own firms, consumers, financial markets, and innovation ecosystem.

creator: Qiyuan Xu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Who Gains in an AI-Supercharged Economy?
content: If AI delivers as promised and fundamentally transforms the economy, we will have moved from the current investment phase, where AI builders dominate, to one where AI users will reap the greatest reward. That is what happens every time an innovation becomes a general-purpose technology.
snippet: If AI delivers as promised and fundamentally transforms the economy, we will have moved from the current investment phase, where AI builders dominate, to one where AI users will reap the greatest
creator: Joseph H. Davis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Development in a Time of Disruption
content: Once firms and economies ascend to market dominance, they often kick away the ladder behind them and erect barriers to market entry to stymie would-be challengers. But developing countries can and do leapfrog incumbent technologies, and conditions for doing so may be particularly favorable now.
snippet: Once firms and economies ascend to market dominance, they often kick away the ladder behind them and erect barriers to market entry to stymie would-be challengers. But developing countries can and do
creator: Keun Lee
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump’s Un-American Capitalism
content: The current US administration's openly corrupt style of crony capitalism is the antithesis of the institutional foundation on which the US economy was built. The winners in the new oligarchic competition are not those who make the best products, but rather those who are best at flattering the mad king.

creator: Joseph E. Stiglitz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can the Manchester Model Save Britain?
content: Following a decades-long effort to lay the foundations for economic dynamism, Greater Manchester has become the UK’s fastest-growing region. While the Manchester model cannot be precisely replicated at the national level, it does carry important lessons for outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer's likely successor.
snippet: Following a decades-long effort to lay the foundations for economic dynamism, Greater Manchester has become the UK’s fastest-growing region. While the Manchester model cannot be precisely replicated
creator: Diane Coyle
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Trillionaires Are Really Made
content: Some may argue that the world’s first trillionaire is a story of technological triumph and a win for American capitalism. But markets have not bet on cutting-edge technology or a company too big to fail, but rather on the idea of a mega-monopolist with wealth and influence so vast that the US government will not allow him to fail.
snippet: Some may argue that the world’s first trillionaire is a story of technological triumph and a win for American capitalism. But markets have not bet on cutting-edge technology or a company too big to
creator: Nabil Ahmed
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cities Show How Climate Action Makes Life Better
content: Cities have shown that a combination of adaptation measures and energy efficiency can save lives, improve well-being, reduce energy costs, and create conditions for sustainable growth. A new partnership seeks to help mayors and city leaders scale up these efforts through technical and financial assistance.

creator: Michael R. Bloomberg
